profile.name: Calvin Hou
profile.title: Electrical Engineer • Robotics
profile.scopeBrief: EE @ Georgia Tech · robotics · PCBs · embedded control. Pick a channel below.

about.paragraph: I'm an Electrical Engineering student focused on building reliable robotics + hardware systems. I enjoy bridging design and implementation—from PCB layout to embedded control.
My work includes thermal analysis for AI hardware, inverse-kinematics research, and electrical system design for competition and racing.

projects.title: Pulse FRC Pit Display
projects.description: Designed and optimized UX/UI for a multi-functional pit display supporting FRC teams during competition. Reached 4,000+ users in the first week.

projects.title: Custom Media Controller & Keyboard
projects.description: Designed and fabricated a custom PCB using KiCad and STM32. Built a keyboard with diode matrix scanning and Teensy USB keyscanning.

projects.title: 3DOF Robotic Arm Controller
projects.description: Implemented a custom controller for a 3DOF arm with inverse kinematics to move the end effector to target coordinates.

experience.title: Research and Design Intern
experience.description: Assembled and validated liquid-to-air cooling solutions for high-performance AI hardware. Conducted thermal analysis and optimized parameters for deployment.

experience.title: Advanced Robotics Centre Research Intern
experience.description: Built and implemented a custom controller for a 3DOF arm, applying inverse kinematics to move an end effector to 3D targets.
